reviews.text:
I think of M. Avery Austin III as “The Lemonade Lawyer”. When his client has been handed a basket of sour lemons, he uses his compassionate listening skills, his in-depth understanding of the criminal justice system, and his extensive experience in the courtroom to make the best lemonade possible. He works hard to help his clients move forward in their journey of life with as little damage as possible to their record. In the case I followed most closely, I was especially impressed with his ability to help his client understand why accepting a particular plea offer was in her best interest and how she could take full advantage of the terms of that agreement. For her, he made “excellent lemonade”.
J.H.S.   Feb 1, 2026
Very poor communication

First and foremost I must send my thanks, gratitude and respect to Mr. Avery Austin for his service and for his passion to always help and assist his fellow Americans no matter the battle
..My son was unfortunately incarcerated in Texas after only being there for 5 days because he was trying to rekindle a past relationship. Things took a wild turn and my son was in a jail 1500 miles away from any family to assist and comfort him during that extreme life changing situation. On came Mr Avery, Mrs.Shannon and his firm that he created to continue to serve his country and the ones less fortunate who otherwise might not have a voice or defense for that matter. Obviously the 4 1/2 month process to have my son released with only a $300 fine after having a charge, mind you a felony; robbery..Mr. Avery Austin orchestrated a deal and ultimately my sons release soon thereafter and for that well forever be grateful to Austin Law PLLC...Any incarceration sucks for all those affected but I can say that if you're represented by the Austin Law firm, your family, friend , etc is in very good hands and all I can say is just be patient. Texas is very backed up statewide and nothing will happen fast as some other states. Please keep that in mind. Do that and you'll end up like my wife, son and I, very happy with the representation Austin Law provided. My son is back home now and very grateful for Mr. Avery and his firm and has been putting his best foot forward daily. Thank you again for everything. We are forever grateful and Mr. Avery, you are a true American Hero!  Forever grateful!!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you so much for your incredibly kind and thoughtful review. Your words mean more to us than we can express. At Austin Law PLLC, we are committed to standing by our clients and their families during some of the most difficult times in their lives. Knowing that we were able to help your son, and bring some peace and resolution to your family, is the very reason we do what we do.

We understand how overwhelming and emotional the legal process can be—especially from across the country—and we truly appreciate your patience and trust in us throughout the journey.

Please know that your family’s strength, faith, and perseverance were just as critical to the outcome. We are so happy to hear your son is home and doing well. That’s what it’s all about.

From all of us at Austin Law PLLC—thank you again for your trust and for sharing your experience. We’re forever grateful for clients like you.

Do not attend mediation with Phylis Offerman if one party is represented by Graham Family Law. She is their preferred mediator and demonstrates clear and obvious bias. Ms. Offerman convened a mediation case that involved coercion and domestic violence -- violating the first principles of fair and balanced mediation. People have paid for multiple mediations with her only to have her very clearly side with the side that enters the conversation represented by Graham Family Law. This is a gross abuse of her position as a mediator. This is shared as a review after I myself took the 40 hour mediation course Texas requires for court-ordered mediators to learn if Ms. Offerman was truly working in alignment with state guidelines and rules. Exceptionally disappointing to see people abuse the mediation process in such a way, especially as mediators are trusted to act a neutral parties who facilitate collaborative resolution --  not whatever Ms. Offerman is pretending to do.
Do not trust him!

This review is long overdue, but it's never too late to let someone know how they blessed your life. Mr. Cantrell and his team helped my son in a sexual assault case. We were not sure at first if he was going to be able to help us. It seemed hopeless under the circumstances. Also, I learned, Mr. Cantrell was super busy and had so many cases. I chose him because of the reviews on the website. But then I also spoke with some people who worked with him personally. The phrase they used was, "Tony is a wolf". LOL! I took that to mean he will go after the prey. As he and his team were preparing for the case it did NOT seem like they had it all together (I'm just being honest here). But when we got to court, MY GOD! I was shook! Mr. Cantrell was stealth. He was so prepared and had truly done his research. He represented my son so well it brought me to tears. And best of all, my son was released and set free. His life has been changed FOREVER! I believe God put this man in our lives. So I am here to say, if you have any doubts, don't. I highly recommend him. He may come across as short and snippy sometimes, but it doesn't really matter if he wins your case. Invest in him.

Jill was the most prolific lawyer I've ever had. Jill took my case and personally worked with me to achieve maximum disability (when the VA denied and denied). She is smart, tenacious and extremely personable. To her, this is not just a job ... it's a calling, and it shows. I was seeking a great lawyer ... and got one. Also got a lifetime friend! I obviously recommend highly.

I tried to deal with the VA myself a couple times. Could never get anywhere, so I contacted Jill. She was able to get my monthly payment increased and back pay. Jill is amazing and fights for what you deserve.

Jill without a doubt provided me the assistance I needed.  I had all but given up on my case.  I injured my back while serving as a paratrooper and like so many others, VA always said "not service connected". Jill took the time to connect the dots in a way I could never have imagined.  She explained my history in a clear concise manner that led to acceptance by VA of my claim.

She is the best!  It took over four years, but we got there.  I can't thank her enough.

Jill went through my medical history, found a secondary condition, and filed my claim. This happened mid-May 2025.
On August 15, 2025, I got my final decision - 100% P&T
All thanks to Jill because she took her time to help me, she kept communicating with me and she contacted me before the VA.

Most lawyers are self-serving and not to be trusted. Look at Washington DC or any major city. All of them ran by lawyers. This is where Ruben James Reyes stands out. He understands the fiduciary relationship. He one hundred percent put my interest above his own. He stood in the fire with me, patiently, and we prevailed. He defended me and the process. The state could not railroad me. Although they tried because it is systematic not personal. Last and definitely not least is his aid.  The best. Hands down. She responsive and her experience is bar none. I will not give her name. Hire The Law Office of Ruben James Reyes and find out. Thank you, James.

reviews.text: As the wife of the defendant in a harrowing self-defense murder case, I cannot praise Larry enough for his extraordinary work. The DA, backed by a full team, was relentless, twisting facts for a conviction rather than seeking truth, but Larry outmatched them single-handedly. By our side for over 20 months of house arrest, he was a steadfast guide, patient and strategic, easing our fears. His jury selection was meticulous, ensuring fairness; he captivated the courtroom, holding the jury’s focus; and his closing argument, with clear PowerPoint visuals, demolished the DA’s false narrative. Without Larry’s brilliance, my husband—innocent but attacked—might be in prison. For self-defense cases, Larry is unmatched. 5 stars! Texas Law Shield set us up with him!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for your heartfelt words, Araceli It was truly an honor to stand by your family through such a difficult and emotional case. I’m grateful for the trust you placed in me and proud that justice prevailed. Your husband’s courage and your family’s strength throughout this process were inspiring. I wish you both continued peace and healing moving forward.— Larry Dean Bloomquist, Attorney at Law

reviews.text: I do not recommend you hire this gentleman. When I hired him,he said my case was winnable.Had a false report put on me and this was just a hearsay case.He seemed like he was working with prosecutors or friends with them. He said prosecutors were going to use my kids against me and that they were witnesses..My kids were not witnesses.When I said I wanted evidence, he never got me anything. He was only worried about his other trials .innocent guy with a conviction now .said was best to take a plea deal..Never did a thing to find evidence I was innocent..Saw his reviews and trusted this guy with my life which was a huge mistake on my part.He was given to me by texas law shield. He even called my only witness and threatened her with jail..everything on report he showed me was a lie, even the car I drove was one I do not own..He said somewhere in there it has your vehicle. Of course it does but that's coming from police. Was yelling at me before court like I was guilty,telling me what I should of done..I should of just fired him.He will probably say I got him a great deal but for being innocent a conviction is Still what it is..Didn't care I'm a hard working dad with 2 beautiful daughters. I will over come this but will never again trust this gentleman .God bless🏀🏀Ridiculous this guy's response.I think you know what you did and how you did absolutely nothing. You scared my only witness threatening her with jail if she helped me.Also I found out cps has no record of my files.I will seek another representative and find out who got rid of those files.Sad he even says here witness wanted to change her report and that is when he scared her off and threatened jail time which is false on his part cause the one getting arrested would have been the victim and she would of been there to testify. Such a bogus attorney and I will be reaching out to the board as well .He stuck me in a small room before we approached the Judge and said I should take plea cause I could get alot of jail time.I wasn't even giving a chance to speak.I told him to pull cps cassette tape of the confession, because she confessed to cps as well and he would not on the multiple times I asked him.
reviews.ownerResponse:
In criminal defense it is impossible to make everyone happy. I am unable to give client's the discovery under state law. I go over the reports and evidence with every client and he mentions seeing the reports in his review. I stand by what I told him. During my investigation one of the witnesses wanted to change the story that she told police on video. I found it unconvincing and did explain to her that if true than her original story would be a false report to a police officer which would be a crime and yes could be prosecuted.

I got his felony charge reduced to a misdemeanor, which I felt was a good deal. As in all cases he had the choice of proceeding to trial or entering the plea. He chose the plea. I am always willing to argue to a jury that someone is not guilty something I have done over 160 times. I never force anyone into taking a plea. If they do so it is there decision after seeing the evidence and my best legal advice after doing this for 22 years. But is always there choice and I am always willing to stand in up and make an argument to a jury.

In order to do a plea a person must fill out papers and be questioned by a judge to ensure the plea is feely and voluntarily made. The judge in this case did that and both the Judge and I believed that this was his decision. If either of us believed anything else we would have proceeded to trial.

I think that this is a case of buyer's remorse. I do wish my former client well. I would also say that in this business dealing with serious matters is is impossible to make everyone happy. Sometimes the easiest thing to do to blame the Attorney for their own actions or the difficulties of the justice system.

reviews.text:
Lifesaver in the Courtroom: Larry's Unmatched Defense Skills

I can't recommend Larry enough—5 stars aren't enough for the lifesaving work he did on my case. Facing a murder charge in self-defense, I was up against a ruthless DA obsessed with a "win" over justice, backed by an entire team of lawyers. For 20 grueling months on house arrest, Larry was my rock: patient, strategic, and utterly brilliant. He dominated jury selection, commanded the courtroom during trial, and sealed it with a powerhouse closing argument using clear PowerPoint slides that dismantled their false narrative. Without Larry's unmatched expertise, I'd be wrongfully imprisoned despite my innocence. If you're in a self-defense bind, Larry is the only choice— he's on another level!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks for the great review! Representing an innocent person is the hardest thing to do. I am so happy and relived that justice was done in your case. Helping innocent people git real justice and the ability to reclaim their lives is the ultimate in job satisfaction.

reviews.text:
Larry was a bedrock of sanity in a process that was otherwise quite unreal and took fully three years.

Larry made sure I always knew what was happening, what was expected to happen next, and how we would approach each specific issue that might arise as we went through the process.

In the end, the DA's office spent three years threatening to try me, and offering a frankly ridiculous plea deal (not that I ever entertained it), before dropping the charges when they realized (with some prompting from Larry himself) that they didn't have a case and probably should have saved us all the time and stress by looking at it properly much earlier.

I was very lucky to have Larry in my corner:  He knew his stuff, he knew the people who would be potentially trying to prosecute me, and he made sure he knew me and the details of everything that occurred.  I didn't have to go to trial, in the end, but I was confident we would have won even if we had, because Larry had covered all the bases and already knew what he needed to say in court.

If you are looking for a lawyer with the experience and expertise to handle your 'use of force' case, whatever the details may be, you simply cannot do better than this credit to the profession.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks so much for your kind words.  I knew you were innocent from the moment we spoke.  I am glad justice was done...even if it took longer than it should.  Good Luck.

reviews.text: My lawyer & my husband had  recently passed away prior to retaining Mr. Bloomquist. I was guilty of violating a 2 yr deferred adjudication. 1 month prior to the end of my probation, I did not show up because I was afraid of my probation officer whom had me arrested 2 times after being hospitalized, I wasn't going for the 3rd & no husband to get me out. I was unaware I could have asked for a new probation officer. I learned all this & much more while I was in prison by a true caring teacher whom I eventually opened up to after a long time keeping to myself, bottling up a lot of frustration & anger! 7 yrs from the day I did not go to probation, it caught up with me. My family hired Mr. Bloomquist & he came to see me in county jail. After we talked about my situation he said not to worry, everything will be ok & I am going to get you out of here.I had believed him & especially so after others whom were criminals, said I would not have to do any time, more probation at most. Never heard from him again, until court in which he then gave me the option of 2 yrs State Jail to satisfy the 2 yrs probation or what the DA offered him which was a reduced time with TDC if I plead guilty to evading arrest, which could not have been proven (lawyer said prior) & serve 18 months. Shocked, I said no, I would rather go before the judge & take my chances. If I am going to prison for 18 months in lieu of 2 years, I might as well take my chances with the judge & hope for leniency due to my situation. I just knew I did not deserve this & something was wrong about this. I had no clue what so ever that I might go to prison! My father & brother just stood there in shock & didn't say anything. I just kept saying no, I will go before the judge. Finally  Mr. Bloomquist spoke up & said he would go talk to the DA again. He came back after a short time with another offer of 12 months. I still said no & after convincing me that it was a good deal & with no prior history & good behavior I was looking at maybe 6 months time & I would be out on parole. WOW, a lot to take in with only a few minutes before I go before the judge. I know now, I made the wrong decision & chose the 12 months & hoped 6 months would go fast. I was out on bond for 3 weeks prior to sentencing & just could not believe this was happening to me. I was numb.I went to prison, 50+ yrs old I worked everyday in there to pass time, took every class that I could, never got in trouble & stayed there for 18 months, not 12 months, not 6 months, 18 months before I was paroled out! My parole officer couldn't even believe I went to prison. It was hell, if there is one. I have been off parole over 2 years now & out of prison over 4 yrs & I am just posting this because of the effects this has brought on my life, I feel like I need to share my experience & hope by doing so it may help others who are ignorant to the system & stay on top of your lawyer.
reviews.ownerResponse:
I am regret that this client was not satisfied with my services. However I believe there are several factual inaccuracies in the review. A public records search indicates she served 1 year and 30 days incarcerated on the two charges not 18 months. She was sentenced to 1 year on the State Jail Felony and has I explain to all my client's there is no early release from state jail and the time must be served day for day. She received a 2 year sentence which is the minimum on the Evading Arrest with a vehicle. I also explain that parole is at the sole discretion of the Department of Criminal Justice and no promises can be made on when it will be granted.

As the reviewed stated above she had absconded from probation for over 7 years. I believe that under the circumstances that a plea for 1 year or half the term was a very good deal considering the length of time she had been gone and the new arrest for felony evading arrest with a vehicle. I also disagree that the Evading Arrest charge could not be proven by the State.

I stand by the deal I struck for this client as in her best interest. No one is happy about being sentenced to prison, and it saddens me every time it happens but it is a reality of the criminal justice system. Sometimes it is easier to blame someone else like the defense attorney, rather than to take
personal responsibility for their own actions. I do wish her the best. I know that I did my best for her in a tough case.

Additionally, no instructions or guidance was given on some very important actions needed after the divorce was complete. This put me in a very bad situation. They were very hands off once the divorce was completed and they were paid. I do not recommend this practice, especially if you are military.

Needing to update a 40+ year old will, who do you call?  Jodie Head-Lopez/Schertz Law Center.  Ms. Lopez-Head and her entire team were SO personable and relatable.  Being an elderly individual, their knowledge and caring guidance and direction was very reassuring and I felt at ease from the very beginning of my will update process. Details and questions I had not thought of or thought about were discussed and explained to my complete understanding.  There is NOTHING like having such peace of mind as you age!

How can I begin to say thank you to Jodi and her team. They make the process of handling tickets or other legal issues you may have a need for extremely easy. Jodi goes above and beyond for her clients. Makes sure you fully understand everything and is always willing to answer questions. She trully treats her clients as if they were her own family.

Would not use anybody else and you shouldn't either!

Jodi Head Lopez was a Godsend to our family! While assisting our son in looking for legal counsel to represent him with his custody case  Ms. Lopez was highly recommended.  This was our first experience with family court so needless to say we were very nervous. After our consultation we were confident we would be in good hands.
Ms. Lopez was able to legally secure our son's rights as a father.
Our family is very thankful for her and her firm. Ms. Lopez is honest and transparent, she is amazing in front of the Judge. Ms. Lopez painted an entire picture for the Judge in court. The Judge was able to hear the entire story and know our family's side of the situation. Ms. Lopez was very responsive to our messages, calls and emails.
Most often she would personally respond unless she was in court.
We are also very appreciative for Marci, Ms. Lopez's paralegal. Marci was always very professional and kind. Marci was easy to get in contact with and always returned our calls or emails most often that same day.
Some Attorneys and Firms are noticeably in it for the money but Ms. Lopez and her team were very respectful of not only our case but our finances as well. You definitely won't get that type of personal client/Attorney relationship with one of those huge law firms.
If you're looking for honest, exceptional, fair and effective legal counsel that truly cares about their clients, LOOK NO FURTHER!!!! HIRE THIS FIRM TO REPRESENT YOU. You will be thankful you did!

I had the pleasure of speaking with Mrs. Jodi Head Lopez last week.  I reached out to her regarding the passing of my dad, who was her client, to handle the estate.  Ms. Lopez, remembered my dad, stating that he was her favorite client.  This meant a lot to me as I know my dad spoke highly of her throughout the years.  As we went through what needed to be done, she provided me with valuable insight, not only for handling his estate, but mine as well and brought up things to consider in keeping an estate from having to be probated. Luckily, my dad had her for his attorney, as he followed all of her advise, alleviating many headaches that can come from being an executor and having to probate a will.  I felt that I have known her for years. I highly recommend Mrs. Lopez, for providing valuable insight, being professional, and having excellent interpersonal skills.

This law firm goes way beyond help!
If you need a lawyer, Jodi is the one to speak to. Every one there is so nice and professional. They respond in a timely manner. I just can't say enough about them. Thank you Jodi and your staff for helping me.

God is real. JODI HEAD LOPEZ. If you get a traffic ticket remember those three words. I spoke to Diego and gave him the basic information. She said she would go into court the next day and talk to the judge. I was skeptical about the whole thing. I put my faith in them. The next day she said judge wouldn't budge but she went the extra mile and looked at the tape and determined my traffic violation was incorrect and got me exactly what I needed done. I paid my dues with Diane and business was done. Everyone I worked with there was lovely and efficient and I don't know how I ended up with them but I swear ill go back (hopefully not but you get the idea).
The building is green, drove right past it.
Took care of my divorce in a professional manner. Very wonderful people.

A divorce involving kids is probably the most stressful thing I have ever had to deal with but Shawn Vandenberg is levels above other attorneys and it really shows.

We live in Lewisville, Texas which is outside of Dallas, Texas, so of course when looking for a law firm to work with us to complete Estate Planning we were given many suggestions.  After meeting with & discussing our needs and assets most were too busy, priced too high for their time or just did not seem too interested in diving into the complex mineral rights with family concerns attached.

I became increasingly frustrated and worried due to age & health concerns I had. I ask  an out of town friend who recommended Chris Ricks of Traeger & Ricks out of New Braunfels. I called and immediately began working with Chris due to his professional attitude and complete understanding of my needs, concerns and complex nature of my situation. I had an idea of exactly what I wanted which he agreed to do for me, but with new laws and his knowledge he spent the time needed to explain to us a very different approach and why it would perform better for our heirs than we had hoped for.

Needless to say we were not only impressed with but trusted this firm completely and am extremely satisfied with their work we have recommended them  to friends and family.  We will only  use Traeger & Ricks for any legal needs in our future.

reviews.text:
If I could leave zero stars I would. Contacted their office today and was told they could offer a consult for $150 which I am more than happy to pay. They said they couldn’t offer any info over the phone which I completely understand. However, they also mentioned that they could not confirm whether or not they could even file the case in the county I would need to file.
When I asked them again to just check to see if they could even practice in that county I was told no, that I would have to come in and pay the consult fee to possibly be told they can’t even begin to help me.
Do they need $150 that badly??? Sad!!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Ms. Stevens,
We appreciate your feedback and would like to know more about your experience with our office so that we can address customer service issues. Could you please submit additional information through our website (www.scaniolaw.com) by filling out the “How Can We Help You?” Box? Please let us know exactly who you spoke with (name, male or female), what you inquired about, and what exactly you were told on the telephone. All clients and potential new clients are important to us and we take this feedback very seriously.
In regards to the counties we serve, we typically take cases in Hays, Comal, Caldwell, and Guadalupe Counties. On rare occasion, we will take a case outside of these counties. We will work with our staff to make sure that this information is clearly communicated to callers on the telephone.
Thank you,
Francesca Scanio Stacey

reviews.text: Julissa did not bother to represent her clients best interest. She cut a deal with DA and refused to fight for her client. Do not recommend. Her only argument is saying “it’s because we are in a conservative county”. Instead of using facts to challenge the DA.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Here is some free education since you do not seem to understand what happened in court yesterday:
1) We had a contested hearing, NOT a plea bargain. Contrary to your review, there was no deal. We argued our case before the court. The prosecutor had zero say in the outcome of the case;
2) As your friend unfortunately learned yesterday, Comal County is one of the most conservative counties in Texas. After hearing the merits of the case, the Judge sentenced your friend to a prison sentence. Anyone who picks up a new criminal charge while on felony probation, is subject to the full range of punishment. Those are simply the facts of life; and
3) The deal offered by the prosecutor was for 6 years prison. After hearing arguments, the judge gave him a 5-year sentence. EVERY criminal lawyer will tell you that is not a loss. Anytime you walk out of the courtroom with less punishment than the state was offering, you WIN! 5 is less than 6….that is very simple math.

And for all of the above-stated facts, YOU ARE WELCOME!

From day one, he went above and beyond not just as an attorney, but as a fierce advocate, a patient listener, and a trusted guide through one of the most difficult chapters of my life.

My case involved a complex administrative process against the federal government, full of red tape and rigid regulations. Aaron jumped in with urgency and took the time to thoroughly understand the regulation exactly as it was written. He broke everything down and made sure every step we took was backed by fact and grounded in the law.

More than anything, Aaron fought for me when others wouldn’t. His belief in my case and in justice made all the difference, and I will forever be grateful for his support. If you’re looking for someone who is not only incredibly skilled, but also genuinely cares Aaron is the attorney you want on your side.

I also want to mention the amazing women in his office, who are always so kind, welcoming, and helpful every step of the way.

Thank you, Aaron. You’ve made a lasting impact on my life, and I will forever be grateful.

I reached out to Lorna after I was caught off guard by a sudden separation agreement. I was overwhelmed, exhausted, and facing tough choices while feeling emotional. Lorna brought genuine compassion and kindness that truly stood out.

She listened to me before anything else. Then she encouraged me to pause and see the situation more clearly. She was honest and reminded me that this was a business negotiation, even though it was painful. That perspective helped me regain control and make better decisions.

Lorna explained the agreement in simple terms, pointed out the most important parts, and guided me on what to ask for and how to do it. I left with a clear plan and much more confidence.

If you ever need someone to stand up for you when things are tough, call Lorna Griffin. She will support you with skill, calm, and real care.

Thank you, Lorna!
Thanks to Lorna and associates, I felt confident and at ease going into the hearing. They walked me through the process, explained what to expect, and prepared me with sample questions beforehand.

I had the privilege of working with Lorna Griffin, a labor attorney in New Braunfels, regarding a complex workplace case. From the very first conversation, she was professional, responsive, and incredibly knowledgeable. Her office provided clear, accurate, and actionable guidance that helped me navigate the situation confidently while remaining fully compliant with the law.

Lorna was on top of everything from day one — she communicated promptly, kept me informed at every step, and provided genuine support throughout the entire process. As a veteran, I deeply appreciate how understanding and supportive she is of those who have served; she truly goes above and beyond to protect the “little guys” who just want fairness and respect in the workplace.

If you’re looking for an honest, highly skilled, and compassionate labor attorney who will fight for you and guide you with integrity, look no further than Lorna Griffin. She’s an absolute gem in her field and I can’t recommend her highly enough.

Lorna Griffin and her team were absolutely outstanding. They took on a very complex, deeply emotional employment matter and handled it with expertise, empathy, and unwavering support. Giselle was amazing—always available, organized, and kind throughout the entire process. Their help brought me peace of mind and a fair resolution. I highly recommend them to anyone facing workplace issues.
Lorna took the time to listen and address my concerns about the document we reviewed.  Did not feel rushed at all.
Working with Loren was great. I really appreciate how far she went for me during my case. Would definitely recommend

I had the privilege of working with Ms. Griffin and her team during a very challenging career transition. From our very first conversation, she was professional, responsive, and candid with her guidance. Rather than over-promising, she took the time to give me a realistic picture of my options and helped me make an informed decision that ultimately turned out to be the best path forward.

Her integrity stood out — she genuinely looked out for my best interest, even when it meant advising me to resolve matters directly rather than spending more on legal fees. That kind of honesty is rare and deeply appreciated.

Because of her advice, I was able to finalize my severance smoothly and use the opportunity as a stepping stone to start my own HR consulting business. What could have been a very difficult chapter became a blessing in disguise, and I credit her thoughtful counsel as a big part of that outcome.

I wholeheartedly recommend Ms. Griffin and her firm to anyone seeking employment law expertise. They bring professionalism, compassion, and clarity when you need it most.

Lorna Griffin did much more than she was required to. She supported and guided me through every step of my case. She was very honest about expectations and time lines. I immediately developed a trust for her recommendations. Overall, I could not have for a better person to represent me.

Lorna Griffin has been a godsend for me, she took my matter seriously, explained exactly what my options were, and treated me with the dignity and respect that I had not received with my previous attorneys.   She saw my case through to the end and I highly recommend her as your first choice in employment attorneys.

I was referred to Ms Griffin from another attorney that was not available for the day I had mediation. I can't thank him enough for referring her. She was definitely a blessing. reviews.ownerResponse:
Navigating employment claims can be a complex and often frustrating process, especially when dealing with sensitive issues like wrongful termination. It’s important to remember that different claims, such as those related to wage disputes, retaliation, or terminations involving protected class statuses (like race, gender, disability, etc.), may be handled by different agencies. For instance, the Texas Workforce Commission (TWC) has a Civil Rights Division that specifically addresses employment discrimination and terminations related to protected class statuses. The U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) also handles employment claims related to protected class statuses. More information about these claims and the roles of the agencies can be found on their websites.

Attorneys do charge for their services, including consultations and any legal representation actions, such as drafting demand letters. Cost for legal representation reflects the time, expertise, and effort involved. In this instance, no formal legal representation was undertaken. We understand how challenging these situations can be and we wish you well in resolving your employment matter.

Once I hired Bryce, everything changed. The relief I felt from not having to deal with the insurance company directly was immeasurable. From his clear guidance to his willingness to take complete control of the case, Bryce handled every detail with confidence and precision. During mediation, he was aggressive, strategic, and unwavering, truly outmatching the insurance company at every turn.

Initially, they offered only $12,000. By the end of the process, even without settling in mediation, Bryce secured over $80,000. Because of him, I was able to fully reconstruct my home and even make upgrades that would have been impossible otherwise.

reviews.text:
This law firm is, without exaggeration, the worst legal experience I have ever had, and in my opinion they completely failed me as clients, professionals, and human beings. I hired them in 2024 for two MTAGS and again in March 2025, I was trusting them with my freedom during a very serious two engage in criminal activity also two aggravated assault cases. While I was incarcerated, they did absolutely nothing of value for my defense. No motions were filed. No bond reduction hearing was requested despite my repeated demands. I was told that doing their actual job would “make the DA and judge mad,” which is an outrageous excuse for pure laziness and fear. I was pressured to accept a 15-year prison sentence without ever being shown discovery, evidence, or any meaningful explanation of my case. Not once did the main attorneys come see me in jail. Communication was so bad that my mother had to relentlessly call, text, and leave voicemails just to get basic updates.

The ONLY attorney who ever came to see me while I was locked up has since left the firm and is now a District Attorney sitting next to the OTHER District Attorney’s in court. Let that sink in. While I was incarcerated and vulnerable, the person representing me is now literally on the other side of the courtroom. In my opinion, I was misled, lied to, and strung along while my life was on the line. After I bonded out, I immediately hired a competent lawyer who removed this firm from my case something that should tell you everything about the quality of representation I received.

When I requested a refund for the complete lack of work done, I was met with intimidation instead of accountability. During an in-person meeting, they pulled out a paper invoice that they conveniently “created,” showed it to me briefly, and then REFUSED to let me take a copy with me. I was told they would email it later, which felt extremely shady and unprofessional. To make matters worse, I was then threatened with a potential $7,000 charge simply for hiring another lawyer “without notifying them,” despite the fact they had already failed to represent me in any meaningful way. Instead of addressing my concerns, I was told to “go to the state bar,” and was even told they’ve been through this exact situation with “plenty of clients” before which speaks volumes about how this firm operates.

In my opinion, this firm takes money, does little to nothing for their clients, avoids responsibility, and then attempts to bully or intimidate clients who question them. The level of dishonesty, incompetence, and disregard for a client’s freedom is shocking. I trusted this firm during one of the darkest times of my life, and they completely abandoned their duty to represent me. If you value your case, your money, or your future, do yourself a favor and stay as far away from this law firm as possible.

Starting off I would say Langley and Banack is the type of firm to take on your case without actually keeping up with it. It seems over extending themselves to any case just to charge for it is their approach. Upon initially meeting with Bill Sommers he reassured me that he would
Do everything possible to ensure I received the claim I was owed. However, he passed my case along to his associate, Thomas Lillibridge, almost immediately. I was left to receive and forward all court notifications to L&B of which I was still charged for filing, though I essentially was doing their job for them. Of course there are the countless instances that I'd reach out to my counsel for updates to which I was always promptly notified that they were working on a much bigger, much more important case. To top it off, after numerous attempts to communicate with my attorney via phone and email, I resorted to dropping in to the office for an update. It apparently made Mr. Sommers quite angry that I did so and he repeatedly exclaimed to me that I shouldn't have stopped by. After resolving the issue with him. I was left to making him aware of the all details regarding my case, that I paid him to manage for me. After numerous attempts to communicate with a board member in regard to the management of my case I am left to leaving this review after paying the tab for all the work I did for them.  It quickly became very clear that neither  I nor my case were of their slightest priority. I hope all their potential clients read this review and forego making the same mistake I did in entrusting Langley and Banack with their representation.
reviews.ownerResponse: At Langley & Banack we take any complaint against our attorneys seriously; nevertheless,  our records indicate that we have no client by the name of Eleo Caper. Any such complaint will be immediately investigated and responded to.

reviews.text: One of the worst experiences I’ve ever had with a lawyer. The first time I ever had to get a lawyer was because my mother was killed in a car accident due to a mailman driving recklessly, so my maternal grandparents sued the postal service. The layers who worked with me then were actually concerned about me. Leaving me orphaned into our messed up foster system that I eventually conquered and aged out of with no problems; I moved on to college and became a home health care provider. One day after I got out of class and on my way to a client’s house I was cut off by an officer driving recklessly in a police suv, not once but twice. I honked at him both times and once we were on a rural road he decides to abruptly stop in front of me and proceeded to get out of his vehicle and confront my vehicle and harass me by asking what my problem was so I told him I didn’t think it was okay for him to cut me off the way he did and speed the way he was. He asked for my information and without hesitation I gave it to him. As soon as I did that he told me I was being detained for tailgating! I was so scared that I called the cops on him and by the time he came back to my vehicle and saw me on the phone, he kept asking for me to get out of my vehicle but I didn’t feel safe and due to my PTSD from childhood sexual trauma I froze and couldn’t/wouldn’t get out so he and 2 other male cops pull me out of my vehicle and got on top of my back and hand cuff me after throwing me around like a rag doll! I was taken downtown and my vehicle was towed, I don’t even think I was read my Miranda rights but I’ve had to to suffer consequences along with rebuilding and going back to counseling because the flashbacks are impending me in my life from being able to function or even go back to school. Let me tell you a short story about this law firm — and no, I’m not a paid actor or part of any scam. My name is Giovanni, and this is my honest experience. After my accident with an 18-wheeler, I wasn’t sure if I should just go through insurance or call a lawyer. Looking back now, I’m extremely glad I chose a lawyer. Even though I live five hours away, this firm never once treated distance as an issue. They truly help people no matter where you’re from.
At the beginning, things were hectic — not because of the firm, but because we were trying to figure out what was medically wrong with me. That’s when I met Mr. Jason Desouza. Let me tell you, that man is not only incredible at his job, but he is a genuine human being who truly cares. I’m jumping around here because he’s helped me in so many ways, not just legally. During this case, my father was sent to the ICU and later passed away. Through all of that, Jason and his team stood by me with real support. I’m honestly emotional even typing this because what they did for me and my family meant the world — especially helping me save my dad’s house. Even if I hadn’t received a settlement (which I did), their compassion alone would have made this experience unforgettable.
The firm has different teams handling every part of your case. On the medical side, Mrs. Cynthia deserves her own spotlight. She is absolutely amazing at what she does. From helping coordinate surgeries, hotels, appointments, and follow-ups — to randomly checking in just to see how I was holding up — she went above and beyond. When she was briefly gone, I instantly realized the difference. Cynthia actually answers, calls back, and genuinely cares about your recovery. She deserves all the respect in the world for how dedicated she is.
Then there’s Mrs. Michelle, the paralegal, who kept me informed every step of the way. Whether it was depositions, case updates, or settlement progress, she always had answers. Hearing her say “congratulations” when the case settled was an unforgettable moment. On top of that, every year they send me a birthday card signed by the entire team — not printed, but hand-signed. That small gesture shows they see you as more than just a case number.
Now let me talk about Mr. Lucas. During my deposition and especially mediation, this man showed up ready to fight. Watching him work was like seeing a parent step in to defend their child — he did not hold back. The entire Desouza team operates like warriors. If you’ve ever seen the movie 300, that’s the energy they bring. They fight hard, stand strong, and don’t back down when it comes to protecting their clients.
Yes, I’m still healing and dealing with future medical care — but that’s what this process is for. They made sure I was taken care of. Long story short: if you’re injured, don’t waste time shopping around. Call this team. Mr. Desouza, Mr. Lucas, Mrs. Cynthia, Mrs. Michelle, Mrs. Jessica, and everyone else involved — thank you all.
This firm does NOT see you as a paycheck. They see you as a human being. I could’ve kept this short, but I wanted anyone reading this to truly understand my experience. I would absolutely choose them again and recommend them without hesitation.

Thank you, Desouza team. It was an honor working with you, and I truly hope we stay in touch
DeSouza took goodcare of me, my case was a bit complicated, and got me the best possible return.

November 2025. Attorney Lauren M. Horne, of the DeSouza Law Firm allegedly overcoached witnesses in
our case, when working for a large Insurance Defense Company in San Antonio, Texas.

Respectful response to DeSouza Law Firm comments below;

First, we have read Goggle Review Policies. We have not harassed or contacted Attorney Horne since we are not writing directly to her. We are not Doxxing Attorney Horne. In sum, we are providing our personal experience with Ms. Horne involving a Fair Housing Act Discrimination Investigation complaint against her Client.

We are a couple with two separate Google Accounts. We thank you for stating we have not written a negative review about your DeSouza Law Firm. Instead, our negative Review applies to your Associate Attorney Lauren M. Horne. We reference our experience when she was an Associate Attorney at Thornton Law Firm, San Antonio, Texas. Hence, we carefully avoided attacking your law firm. However, you have decided to criticize us for providing lawful opinions regarding Attorney Horne's alleged unethical performance in a case you are not privy to. We also filed a Motion to Disqualify Attorney Horne with the Court and her previous law firm prior to your law firm hiring her. That is not libel.

We have followed Goggle Guidelines. We have provided lawful opinions on Attorney Lauren Horne. We employ the word " Alleged."

As you are aware,  individuals have four years to forward alleged State Bar of Texas Bar Ethics complaints against Attorneys. We have stated Attorney Lauren Horne allegedly engaged in overcoaching of witnesses and other alleged State Bar of Texas Ethics  violations.

For example, we have the TWC- HUD transcripts where Attorney Horne interjected and interrupted her client while he was testifying. She used the same wording she allegedly over-coached other Respondent Clients to repeat under oath to a State Investigator. In our opinion, that is an alleged violation of the Texas Disciplinary Rules of Professional Conduct.

Advising consumers of an Attorney's alleged unethical behavior is not desperate. We have nothing to be desperate about. We are merely reporting our experience with Attorney Horne. We are alerting potential clients to Attorney Horne's alleged unethical conduct. Again, that endeavor is hardly desperation?

Periodically we update our original Goggle Review on Attorney Horne when we receive new information. For example, we have learned her client was recently fired by the City of Floresville for "25 issues," that included misuse of City of Floresville Government and Charitable Trust funds and alleged embezzlement of State and Federal funds. The Wilson County News provided several articles on her fired Client.

Finally, we will correct any alleged Goggle Review Guideline error on our part. We will also remove our review of Attorney Horne if and when the Texas State Bar finds she violated no Professional Code of Conduct or Ethics violations. That is fairness.

Let us know if you file a Grievance with Goggle. This due process procedure permits us to lawfully respond to your allegations.

Again, below is our respectful opinion and review on Attorney Horne. Truth and opinions are not defamation or libel. This is why we have websites like Goggle. To alert the publc on Attorneys legal services and their respective performance therein.

Here is our opinion and review on Attorney Lauren Horne:

reviews.text: I want to acknowledge that the team members I’ve interacted with have always been professional and kind. The ongoing lack of communication from the firm has made it challenging for me to remain hopeful about receiving compensation for my suffering. I had initially hoped for a resolution, but I’m left feeling disheartened by this prolonged process. If it has to take this long, at the very least the firm should improve their communication. Had an amazing experience! Mailed was very professional and communicative! He won my case! And a case I had spoken to numerous lawyers previously that didn’t want to take my case. Did an amazing job! Sent so many examples of previous case law that bolstered my case. He had great tips for things that I could do personally to strengthen my case. Overall an amazing experience for a difficult time in my life!

I can't express in totality my appreciation for the person, performance,  law acumen, truthfulness and guidance at every stage of my case by attorney Michael Baseluos.  I never imagined that I'd need an attorney.  However, life has a way of bringing challenges that demand the need for professionals in the field of law.

In 2017, I acquired CIDP, a autoimmune disorder as the result of a flu vaccine.  I had originally consulted with a different firm that marketed themselves as the industry leader in vaccine related injuries.  Over the course of 2 years I was instructed that nothing could be done and for me to abandon the case.

I was rather devastated in that I was led to believe that we would easily settle the case.  Now I was being asked to live with deficits caused by the vaccine injury.  What I believe to be true is that once it was realized that a lot of work is required in settling these types of cases, the other firm wasn't up to the challenge.

I began calling and leaving messages for attorneys all over the USA to see if anyone would be willing to pick up the pieces of a case that had been fumbled for over 2 years.

Thankfully, Mr. Baseluos returned my call.  In short order he began to lay out the plan and process with integrity, truthfulness and transparency.  The acumen in which he utilized his craft in securing expert witnesses, utilizing videographers to display the depth of my deficits and in just keeping me calm in the midst of the storm was remarkable.

There were times when I wanted and pressed to hear what I wanted to hear in regard to the process.

Mr. Baseluos never waivered and always stayed true to the law with full transparency.  I've often said that he represented me as if he were representing himself.  It really felt that it was indeed personal.

Through Mr. Baseluos reputation, expertise and guidance we were able to settle my case in which I am grateful.  I don't believe that anyone else could have or would have done what he did for my case.  For that, I am extremely thankful.  I searched across the USA for a reputable, honest, knowledgeable attorney with integrity and acumen in the field of law, only to find one in my backyard in San Antonio, Tx

There's literally only one thing that I wish I had done differently.  I wish that I had found and consulted Mr. Baseluos on my team from day 1.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for these wonderful words. Hearing such comments reminds me of why I became an attorney. I only wish I could have done more. You were an amazing client and I thoroughly enjoyed getting to know you and share in your experience. I wish you and your family all of God's blessings in life. All the best, Mike

reviews.text:
I am certain it was due to the talent, wisdom, and dedication of Mr. Baseluos that we won my case. It was difficult, long, and unique, but none of that hindered him.

I lost about half of my vision due to an autoimmune response to a vaccine and am now legally blind. There is a program to compensate vaccine injuries and I hired Mr. Baseluos to represent me through this vaccine court. I initially considered representing myself but now I know that would have been a failing effort. In the vaccine court, the government can be very contentious and petitioners with injuries are often unsuccessful because many cases can't be "proven" to satisfaction of the court. Mr. Baselous was familiar with the unique format of the vaccine court and the specialized rules for the program. He is one of the select attorneys listed as qualified to work in the program and, I believe, he's the only one in the San Antonio area.

During my case, the government argued especially aggressively against my claim. Previously, it had never been established whether or not the influenza vaccine could cause optic neuritis. Mr. Baseluos identified approximately 20 prior claims in the program and each of them had been settled with the government without any ruling as to whether the injury could be caused by the vaccine. Due to my young age, successful career, and significant injury, our claim was for a large sum of money - primarily lost future earnings. I believe this is why the government contested my claim whereas it settled with all who had come before me.

We had no specific precedence to rely upon but Mr. Baseluos assembled a team of extremely specialized experts on my behalf. This included a neuro-ophthalmologist from Baltimore, a renowned immunologist specializing in molecular mimicry from the University of Southern California, a radiologist specializing in eyes and brains, and others. Mr. Baseluos spent more than five years coordinating the efforts of this team to argue my case. He reviewed dozens of scientific reports from both parties as well as hundreds of referenced scientific studies. Ultimately, Mr. Baseluos presented a strong argument, based on extremely technical scientific data, and backed by an army of acclaimed medical and scientific experts, which showed that the influenza vaccine, through a process called molecular mimicry, is capable of triggering an autoimmune response that can lead to optic neuritis. The argument was so strong and complete that the court accepted it and rejected the counter-argument by the government and its scientific experts.

Mr. Baseluos set a precedence in the vaccine court. Unlike us, every following case of influenza vaccine induced optic neuritis will be able to point to a previous ruling in favor of the injured person.

During our arguments over financial damages, Mr. Baseluos was equally as committed and competent as he had been during our fight over causation. He brought aboard an expert accountant, a Medicare and medical insurance specialist, and other experts. The arguments over financial damages were frustrating to me because they seemed to be even more obvious than the causation, but Mr. Baseluos systematically refuted the government's arguments and, ultimately, we agreed to an amount of money I believe was fair.

During this case, Mr. Baseluos  worked tirelessly on my behalf. We spoke late into the night regularly as he was finalizing reports or preparing for the multi-day hearings in the case. Mr. Baseluos was passionately devoted to my case. I cannot imagine another attorney being so invested or working so diligently.

He took on and beat the government, their scientific experts, and their limitless resources.

He set precedence in the vaccine court.

He steadfastly represented me for over half a decade.

The lady said it was too late, there wasn't anything they could do about it. We found Mike Baseluos, he said she still had a shot. Not only did he bring hope for my mother, he kept us close to what was going on during the process of her claim. The amount we were expecting was doubled in 2022. She quickly sent me information on Jason Barger with The Barger Law Firm. She had also mentioned to Jason at the Chamber luncheon and that I would be calling to set up an appointment. I contacted his office and quickly got an appointment with him. I sat down with Jason and went over the details that I wanted in my Will and my Power of attorney and Power of medical attorney. Jason‘s professionalism and compassion were outstanding. He guided me through the things that were necessary to be placed in the documents. reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. Villagran,
At our practice, we take client concerns seriously and are committed to providing professional, ethical, and effective legal representation. We understand that legal matters can be emotionally and financially stressful, and we strive to offer transparent communication and fair billing practices throughout the process. As indicated throughout our contract, we permit clients to inquire as to their invoices and provide clarification.
We’re also proud of our team’s dedication, we would like to clarify that having more than one attorney involved is often a strategic decision to ensure that each case receives the attention and support it deserves- which was disclosed and made known to you.

We have often met with you, and others, outside of scheduled appointment times to address concerns in each case. We have often had to come in before and stay far beyond office hour times to assist you. We appreciate your recognition of Juliana’s efforts and are glad to hear she made a positive impact – and that a difficult outcome was achieved which removed a lot of concerns you had regarding the prior order.
As the Judge permitted us to mention at the withdrawal hearing, at the time of our withdrawal you had an outstanding balance of over $8000 to the Firm. Thus, we respectfully disagree with your assertion that the firm, or any of its members, “did not value your case”. Achieving difficult results is often an uphill battle and requires days of work. It is unfortunate in this case, that you want to reap the benefits of our hard work and not pay for the time it took to achieve them. As previously mentioned, we will be seeking legal remedies in this case for both the nonpayment and untruthful statements made by you.

reviews.text: Juliana Johnson and the team at McNutt Law Firm have been outstanding. From our initial consultation she provided clear guidance, genuine care, and demonstrated her knowledge and experience in challenging child custody suits. Even though our case is still ongoing, we have already experienced positive progress and tremendous relief thanks to her dedication and responsiveness. Juliana has shown how she fights for her clients in court, and her professionalism and compassion have made a stressful process much easier to navigate.

Honest Review of The McNutt Law Firm

I hired The McNutt Law Firm in 2024 to handle my custody case, and it turned out to be one of the worst decisions I’ve ever made.

My daughter’s mother had spent years trying to keep me out of her life. Since her birth in 2018, we had been in and out of court, and I had already worked with 4–5 different law firms—spending over $25,000 just to gain small steps forward. Even then, her mother still had nearly full control of all rights and duties.

When I hired McNutt Law, they quickly filed for a temporary restraining order that finally allowed me to spend more meaningful time with my daughter. At first, I thought this was a turning point. But my experience with the firm quickly revealed otherwise.

Outrageous Billing Practices

From the beginning, they told me it would cost about $8,000 to get through temporary orders. By the end, I had spent over $45,000—and still did not have final orders—because they filed to withdraw before finishing the job.

Their billing was excessive and unreasonable. I was nickeled and dimed for everything: hundreds of small charges, $5–$10 here, $15 there, just for short emails or quick phone calls. Instead of receiving consistent monthly invoices, they only sent bills every 2–3 months, each filled with vague line items and inflated totals. Whenever I questioned these charges, they didn’t explain—they accused me of being “abusive.”

On top of that, they sent two attorneys to every hearing and billed me for both, even though only one was needed. Worse, they failed to address important issues in the temporary orders, which forced us back into court later—and I was billed again for their mistakes.

Financial Strain and Retainer Trap

They put me on a $2,000/month retainer directly drafted from my business checking account. After nearly a year of paying, I was still over $12,000 in the hole. Eventually, I had to drain money I had saved for a down payment on a home just to keep up with their fees.

Even after paying tens of thousands, they made a clerical error on a pre-authorization for my final payment. Instead of owning the mistake, they harassed me, accused me of lying, and threatened to remove any discounts and sue me for more money—all over a $5,000 balance after I had already paid them more than $45,000.

Negligence and Lack of Support

While I did receive credit for my back child support (minus interest, about $10,000), McNutt Law refused to help update the figures with the Attorney General. That refusal directly prevented me from getting a passport and delayed me from qualifying for a mortgage loan.

When I asked them to help with the Attorney General, they sent me on a wild goose chase—telling me to call myself, only for me to spend hours on hold before learning that the OAG couldn’t even speak to me because McNutt was my attorney of record. When I asked for a small billing adjustment to reflect my wasted time and lost wages, I was once again accused of being “abusive.”

Disrespect in Court

To make matters worse, when things fell apart, attorney Melysa openly disrespected me in court—asking Judge Hays to “warn me about court procedures” and even implying I was being violent. It was unprofessional, humiliating, and completely unnecessary.

The Bottom Line

At one point, the firm even told me I was “ungrateful” because they had secured custody for me. The truth is this: given the facts of my case—including the mother’s involvement with a registered sex offender—ANY competent attorney could have achieved the same results, at a fraction of the cost.

McNutt Law did nothing exceptional. Instead, they exploited my situation, drained my finances, and walked away before finishing the job.

Stay far away from The McNutt Law Firm. They will nickel-and-dime you, drag cases out unnecessarily, and turn on you the moment you start questioning their billing.

Jonathan was responsive, explained legal processes in clear terms, and made sure I understood my options at every stage.

His attention to detail, calm but assertive approach, and willingness to stand his ground were invaluable in achieving a favorable outcome. Jonathan is not easily intimidated and would not allow the opposing attorney to bully or derail the process. He handled a very stressful and complex matter with professionalism and empathy, ensuring my voice was heard and my rights were protected.

If you’re looking for an attorney who combines legal skill, and the tenacity to stand firm, Jonathan Jordan is an excellent choice.

I believe he did this so he could better represent them and I believe in the end he had a reasonable resolution and outlined a workable agreement for both parties. He was very open in communicating when the meetings would take place and was very accommodating to both my ex and myself in scheduling the meetings. His dedication to this case was unmatched to any attorney Ive worked with previously. Mr. Jordan didn't take anyone's side he was truly thinking of what would be in the best interest for my children. He listened to what my children had to say and took that into consideration when making his final decision.  The McNutt Law firm was accommodating during this difficult time. I never felt as if I was being pushed to the side or left out of the loop as they had open communicationfrom the beginning. I received emais, text messages,  and phone calls in a timly manner. This team is very efficient and I wish I had chose them to represent me. reviews.text: Rose and her team (especially Jeannette) are astounding to work with! Rose is extraordinarily diligent, skilled, and knowledgeable in all aspects of family law. She was the only resource that actually fought for the best interests of my family in a very demanding, difficult divorce process. She is level headed, accommodating, and patient with her clients. She went above and beyond expectations to ensure what was best for all parties involved while maintaining a healthy balance of expectations between both parties. I couldn’t imagine having a better relationship with this law firm compared to others in New Braunfels. They will go above and beyond to ensure the wellbeing, safety of your family. Rose is a diamond in the rough for this area.They will not waste your hard earned money on unnecessary fees or motions. They are one of the only law firms that are here for you not a paycheck. Highly recommend Rose and Jeannette as your team!
